data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260511T013402-7aa571bc/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260511T013402-7aa571bc/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260511T013402-7aa571bc/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260511T013402-7aa571bc/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260511T013402-7aa571bc/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-dc3eaa9d">
    <title>feat(bead): pre-claim AC quality lint — reject ill-formed beads before any attempt</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Today a bead can enter execution with ACs that are mostly prose
  ("improve clarity", "logs become cleaner") — items that the
  ac-check tool (ddx-c739d7ec, now landed) cannot mechanically
  verify. Such beads burn drain budget on attempts whose outcome
  the reviewer cannot mechanically gate, then often land via
  rubber-stamp because there is nothing concrete to verify.

  Upstream prevention is cheaper than downstream catching: run
  ac-check against the BEAD'S OWN DEFINITION (no attempt diff) at
  intake or pre-claim time. Compute the fraction of ACs that
  classify as something other than `prose`. If too many are
  prose, the bead is "low-quality AC" and gets parked or routed
  to an auto-reframer for refinement BEFORE any drain attempt.

CURRENT STATE
  - ddx-c739d7ec (ac-check tool) is landed and exposes
    classification of acceptance criteria.
  - cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go runs a pre-claim
    readiness check on each bead but does not assess AC
    mechanical-verifiability.
  - cli/cmd/bead_create.go has no AC quality gate; any AC text
    can be filed.
  - The 8-criterion rubric in docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md
    is documented but not mechanically enforced.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a pre-claim AC quality lint that runs ac-check
  classification against the bead's Acceptance text (no diff
  context — pure classification) and computes a "mechanical
  verifiability score":

    verifiable_count   = items classified as test-name | symbol |
                         negative | build-gate | mechanical
    prose_count        = items classified as prose
    score              = verifiable_count / total

  Default threshold: score >= 0.5 (at least half of ACs must be
  mechanically classifiable). Operator can override via
  .ddx.yml config.

  Behavior:
    1. cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go: before claiming a
       bead, call accheck.ParseAcceptance + accheck.Evaluate
       (with no-op hooks for evaluator — we only need
       classifications, no actual test runs).
    2. Compute the verifiability score.
    3. If score < threshold, the bead is ineligible: set
       Extra["execution-eligible"] = false, add label
       "ac-quality:needs-refinement", emit a
       "ac-quality-low" event with the per-AC classification.
    4. After sister bead "auto-reframe + auto-decompose"
       (ddx-63155d5c) lands, low-AC-quality beads get
       automatically routed to the reframer to improve their
       AC text BEFORE any execution attempt.

  Optional CLI: `ddx bead lint <id>` for ad-hoc invocation —
  prints the per-AC classification and the verifiability score
  so operators can self-check before filing.

  Tests:
    - TestPreClaimACQuality_AllMechanicalACs_Passes
    - TestPreClaimACQuality_AllProseACs_BlocksClaim
    - TestPreClaimACQuality_MixedACs_ScoreAboveThreshold_Passes
    - TestPreClaimACQuality_MixedACs_ScoreBelowThreshold_Routes
    - TestPreClaimACQuality_LowQualityEmitsACQualityEvent
    - TestBeadLint_CLI_PrintsClassification

NON-SCOPE
  - Auto-refining low-quality ACs (sister bead ddx-63155d5c
    handles auto-reframe at the recovery stage; this bead
    detects the problem at intake; the reframer fixes it).
  - Authoring assistance UI (out of scope; this is the
    enforcement gate).
  - Bumping ac-check classification rules to be more
    permissive (separate concern; this bead reads the
    existing classifier).

INTERSECTIONS
  - DEPENDS ON: ddx-c739d7ec (ac-check tool) — landed.
  - Sister bead ddx-63155d5c (auto-reframe) — once landed, the
    "route low-quality to reframer" path becomes automatic.
  - Sister bead ddx-a9ff01a1 (reviewer ratifies ac-check) —
    same tool used at the gate is now used at intake. Single
    source of truth.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go (or a sibling) computes a verifiability score using accheck classification; the score is verifiable_count / total ACs.
2. Default threshold is 0.5; configurable via .ddx.yml under intake.ac_quality.min_score.
3. When score < threshold: bead's Extra["execution-eligible"] = false, label "ac-quality:needs-refinement" added, "ac-quality-low" event emitted with per-AC classifications in the body.
4. `ddx bead lint <id>` CLI subcommand prints the per-AC classification and verifiability score, with --json flag for structured output.
5. TestPreClaimACQuality_AllMechanicalACs_Passes, TestPreClaimACQuality_AllProseACs_BlocksClaim, TestPreClaimACQuality_MixedACs_ScoreAboveThreshold_Passes, TestPreClaimACQuality_MixedACs_ScoreBelowThreshold_Routes, TestPreClaimACQuality_LowQualityEmitsACQualityEvent, TestBeadLint_CLI_PrintsClassification all pass.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:bead, kind:feature, prevention, spec:ADR</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="c96112552ba1e191963daf6b570f9f0902d06cf8" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260511T013402-7aa571bc"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 7 ACs verified:

1. **AC #1** — `cli/internal/agent/acqualitylint.go` (`ComputeACQuality` + `RunPreClaimACQualityLint`) computes `verifiable_count / total` score via `accheck.ParseAcceptance`.
2. **AC #2** — Default threshold `0.5` in `DefaultACQualityMinScore`; configurable via `intake.ac_quality.min_score` in `types.go` / `resolved.go` / schema.
3. **AC #3** — When score < threshold: sets `Extra["execution-eligible"] = false`, adds `"ac-quality:needs-refinement"` label, emits `"ac-quality-low"` event with JSON body.
4. **AC #4** — `ddx bead lint <id>` in `cli/cmd/bead_lint.go` prints per-AC classification and score; `--json` outputs structured `lintOutput`.
5. **AC #5** — All 6 named tests pass.
6. **AC #6** — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` green.
7. **AC #7** — `lefthook run pre-commit` passes (all checks ✔).
